name: Golf
description: The courses, the majors, and the legends of golf

questions.q: How many holes are on a standard PGA golf course?

source.notes: Standard since the 1764 'Act of Parliament' at St Andrews Old Course.

questions.q: How wide in diameter is a regulation golf hole?

source.notes: 108 mm exactly. Set by Royal and Ancient Golf Club in 1891.

questions.q: Which famous garment is awarded to the winner of The Masters?

source.notes: Tradition started 1949. Winners become honorary Augusta National members.

questions.q: What is the term for the ideal number of strokes to complete a hole?

source.notes: A full course is typically par 72. The term 'par' comes from the late-1800s usage meaning 'equality' or 'equal value' (originally a stock-market term). audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed false 'professional average result' backronym.

questions.q: What is one stroke under par on a hole called?

source.notes: Eagle is 2 under par, albatross is 3 under.

questions.q: What is one stroke over par called?

source.notes: Double bogey is 2 over, triple bogey 3 over.

questions.q: What event is the biennial golf match between teams from the US and Europe?

source.notes: First played 1927. Named after English businessman Samuel Ryder.

questions.q: How many rounds does a standard PGA tournament consist of?

source.notes: Thursday through Sunday. 72 holes total.

questions.q: At what age did Tiger Woods win his first Masters Tournament in 1997?

source.notes: Youngest Masters winner at the time. Won by 12 strokes — record margin.

questions.q: Which four tournaments make up the golf 'Grand Slam' majors?
choices: Masters, US Open, Open Championship, PGA Championship
Masters, US Open, Ryder Cup, Presidents Cup
US Open, Open Championship, Players, PGA
Masters, US Open, European Open, PGA

source.notes: The Masters is the only major played at the same course every year (Augusta National).

questions.q: Where is the Masters Tournament held every year?

source.notes: Augusta, Georgia. Founded by Bobby Jones. First tournament 1934.

questions.q: In what year was The Masters Tournament first played?

source.notes: Originally called the 'Augusta National Invitation Tournament.'

questions.q: Rory McIlroy represents which part of the United Kingdom?

source.notes: Born Holywood, County Down, 1989. Won Masters in 2025 to complete career Grand Slam.

questions.q: Which course in Scotland is considered the 'home of golf'?

source.notes: Oldest golf course in the world. Hosts The Open Championship in rotation.

questions.q: What is a hole-in-one on a par 4 hole called?

source.notes: Three under par on a single hole. Also called 'double eagle' in the US. (A hole-in-one on a par 5 is 4 under par and is called a 'condor'.) audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed 'or par 5' from stem because that scenario is a condor, not an albatross.

questions.q: Which club is typically used for the longest distance off the tee?

source.notes: Also known as the 1-wood. Largest head, longest shaft.

questions.q: What is a 'birdie'?

source.notes: Term originated in 1899 at Atlantic City Country Club.

questions.q: The 'Claret Jug' trophy is awarded at which major championship?

source.notes: Oldest of the four majors. First played 1860 at Prestwick Golf Club, Scotland.

questions.q: What is Jack Nicklaus's famous nickname?

source.notes: Won a record 18 major championships. Tiger Woods has 15 majors.

questions.q: Which golfer's nickname is 'The Shark'?

source.notes: Australian golfer. Spent 331 weeks as world #1. Now leads LIV Golf.
